name: kravhantering
description: En webbapplikation för kravhantering som stödjer företagets kravmodell och kravprocess
author: Viscalyx

scripts.check: npm run type-check && npm run lint:py && npm run format:check && npm run spell:check && npm run lint && npm run lint:md && npm run test
scripts.fix: npm run format && npm run lint:fix && npm run lint:md:fix && npm run spell:fix
scripts.test:integration:all: npm run test:integration && npm run test:integration:preview
scripts.clean:all: rimraf .next out .open-next .wrangler && echo 'Run npm run db:setup to restore the local database'
scripts.purge:install: rimraf node_modules && npm cache clean --force && npm install && rimraf package-lock.json && npm install
scripts.db:migrate: yes | wrangler d1 migrations apply kravhantering-db --local
scripts.db:seed: node drizzle/seed.ts > /tmp/seed.sql && wrangler d1 execute kravhantering-db --local --file=/tmp/seed.sql
scripts.db:setup: npm run db:reset && npm run db:migrate && npm run db:seed

//purge:install: Two-phase install: first install rebuilds node_modules with a (possibly corrupt) lockfile,
then the lockfile is deleted and regenerated with node_modules present.
This works around an npm bug where platform-specific optional deps (e.g. @esbuild/openharmony-arm64)
are written as 'extraneous' instead of 'optional' when node_modules is absent during resolution,
which causes 'npm ci' to fail with EBADPLATFORM in CI.
//overrides: Overrides pin transitive dependencies that still resolve to vulnerable versions.
Remove each override when the parent dependency updates to include the fix natively.
Document below this. Example: @swc/helpers: next@16.1.6 pins 0.5.15, but next-intl's @swc/core@^1.15.2 peer-requires >=0.5.17. Override to 0.5.19 resolves the conflict.

fast-xml-parser: @aws-sdk/xml-builder@3.972.11 (via @opennextjs/cloudflare > @opennextjs/aws) pins 5.4.1, which is vulnerable to GHSA-8gc5-j5rx-235r and GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g. Override to 5.5.9 resolves both.
smol-toml: markdownlint-cli2@0.22.0 pins 1.6.0, and cspell-config-lib@9.7.0 still ranges ^1.6.0; 1.6.0 is vulnerable to DoS GHSA-v3rj-xjv7-4jmq. Override to 1.6.1 resolves it.
